prompt: Add proper error handling to the checkout charge code using the see() error framework.
note: ROUTING-ONLY: see() is a code pattern, not a backend resource, and code edits aren't observable under the MCP-only lockdown. This case just confirms an error-handling ask routes to the see skill.

prompt: Migrate these console.error calls to the shipeasy see() error primitive.
note: routing-only: confirms a console.error→see() migration ask routes to the see skill

prompt: The payment capture call can throw a CardError when the bank declines and right now we just swallow it in the catch. Log this error.
note: routing-only: given error context + a terse 'log this error', the agent should reach for the see() error primitive (the actual code edit isn't observable under the MCP-only lockdown).

prompt: How many times does this checkout charge error actually fail in the last 24h?
note: see() occurrences land in the ops queue / Errors dashboard as error-type items — a frequency question should pull them via ops_list (filtered --type error / by window), i.e. read the analytics rather than guess.
